verdict_reason: The engineering layer makes no claim that the Gita is a tech manual. The translation explicitly frames the analog as a derivative reading — 'The engineering layer must hold the harshness. A senior shipping from buddhi-yoga produces better engineering...' — not as the verse's own claim. Sanskrit terms (buddhi-yoga, kṛpaṇa, phala-hetavaḥ, śaraṇam) are preserved and named; the metaphysical floor (Shankara's saṁsāra-causation, Ramanuja's mokṣa-direction, Gaudiya devotional reading) is recorded in the source pack and not collapsed into 'productivity advice'. The analog stays at the operator-state level and explicitly acknowledges (via cross-reference to 18.66) that 'buddhau śaraṇam' is an early-chapter form of a doctrinally larger śaraṇāgati grammar — preventing the engineering reading from claiming to be the verse's terminal meaning. Pass.
